signals.idea: Cleanup and quality pass on timeline agent + gallery selection system. Three objectives: (1) ENGINEERING QUALITY: Review and refactor all code touched across the three recent features (timeline media selection context, agent task creation, gallery selection + multi-drag). Ensure excellent modular decomposition, clear responsibility boundaries, right levels of abstraction, no confusion about what happens where. This spans: useSelectedMediaClips, GallerySelectionContext, useLassoSelection, useModifierKeys, AgentChat attachment merging, useAgentSession send pipeline, create-task tool, clips.ts, generation.ts, tool-calls.ts, loop.ts, prompts.ts, useExternalDrop multi-drag, dragDrop.ts multi-drag type, MediaGalleryItem selection/drag changes, GenerationsPaneGallery selection wiring. (2) BUG FIX: Gallery selection highlighting (ring-2 ring-sky-400) is broken — it appears inconsistently, sometimes shows for a second then disappears, disappears on hover even when item is clicked/selected. This points to some state management or CSS issue in how isSelected prop flows through MediaGalleryItem or how the selection state interacts with hover/interaction states. Investigate and fix. (3) FEATURE: Make the AI timeline agent panel show over the locked pane state as well (currently it only shows when pane is unlocked).

unresolved_flags.concern: agent-chat-rendering: Plan analysis still frames the locked-pane bug around a generic transformed ancestor and does not explicitly account for `pane.transformClass` sliding the whole editor pane off-screen.

agent-tool-utils: Deduplication scope misses the actual shared helpers in `tools/create-task.ts` and assumes client/server sharing is impossible even though the repo already shares cross-runtime modules.

verification: Planned validation omits existing direct tests for the touched frontend files and the final `npx vitest run` command excludes `supabase/functions/**`, leaving the edge refactor largely unverified.
drop-handling: The proposed `createVisualTrackAtTop` extraction does not match all current callers because file drops create both audio and visual tracks depending on file type.

agent-chat-refactor-threshold: Step 10 proposes splitting `AgentChat.tsx` largely on line count even though the repo's own refactoring guide says sub-600-line components should not be split on size alone.

Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Checked `src/features/editor/components/EditorPaneTab.tsx:146-155`, `src/shared/hooks/useSlidingPane.ts:244-278`, and `src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorShell.tsx:420,447`. The plan keeps the right portal fix, but it does not absorb the critique correction that the locked-pane failure is driven by `pane.transformClass` moving the entire editor pane to `-translate-y-full`/`translate-y-full`, not just by a generic transformed ancestor creating a fixed-position containing block.

Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Checked `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/create-task.ts:24-28` and `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/generation.ts:22-32`. The task idea explicitly includes the create-task tool, but the plan's dedupe step is framed around `generation.ts` and `llm/messages.ts`, so it misses the actual duplicated `asTrimmedString` and `asPositiveNumber` helpers that currently live in `create-task.ts` and `generation.ts`.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked `src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem.tsx:352-353`. The plan's proposed replacement class `transition-[shadow,opacity,transform]` is not targeting the actual CSS property Tailwind is animating here, which is `box-shadow`; as written, that fix is underspecified and may not stop the ring/shadow interaction it is trying to isolate.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAgentSession.ts:32-92`, `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/command-parser.ts:1`, and `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/llm/messages.ts:1-9`. The plan says client and edge validation utilities cannot be shared because of different runtimes, but this repository already shares TypeScript modules across that boundary, so that assumption is technically too strong and leaves avoidable duplication in place.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked the three track-creation branches in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts:214-223`, `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts:237-246`, and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts:299-309`. The proposed helper name `createVisualTrackAtTop()` does not fit the third caller, which creates either `A*` or `V*` tracks based on the dropped file type, so that extraction would not correctly cover all current behavior without a kind parameter.

Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: Checked adjacent tests and related selection code in `src/shared/components/MediaGallery/components/__tests__/MediaGalleryGrid.test.tsx`, `src/shared/contexts/GallerySelectionContext.test.tsx`, `src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.test.tsx`, and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAgentSession.test.tsx`. The plan's named work items focus on only part of the affected surface even though these files already encode related selection propagation, send-pipeline, and chat behavior that would also be at risk.

Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: Checked the edge-tool scope in `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/create-task.ts`, `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/generation.ts`, and `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/loop.ts`. Because the original idea explicitly includes the create-task tool and the loop dispatches into it, a refactor plan that treats only `generation.ts` as the server-side helper cleanup target is narrower than the actual subsystem boundary.

Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: Checked existing direct tests for the touched areas. The repository already has `src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.test.tsx`, `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.test.tsx`, `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAgentSession.test.tsx`, `src/shared/components/MediaGallery/components/__tests__/MediaGalleryGrid.test.tsx`, and `src/shared/contexts/GallerySelectionContext.test.tsx`, but the plan's validation list does not include them.

Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?: Checked `config/testing/vitest.config.ts:13-20`. The plan's final gate of `npx vitest run` will not cover any edge-function work because the default config includes only `src/**/*.test.{ts,tsx}` and explicitly excludes `supabase/functions/**`, so the proposed verification does not actually touch `ai-timeline-agent` changes.

Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: Checked `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts:379-454`. `handleAssetDrop` is called with materially different argument shapes across multi-generation, single-generation, file-upload, and asset-key paths, including `trackId` intentionally being `undefined` to force new-track creation and `insertAtTop` only being forwarded for certain cases, so the dispatcher refactor has to preserve those caller-specific semantics exactly.

Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?: Checked the send pipeline caller contract in `src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx:241-259` and its existing assertion in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAgentSession.test.tsx:43-84`. Refactoring `useAgentSession.ts` without explicitly keeping that caller contract in the verification plan risks silently changing how `generation_id` is serialized for selected attachments.

Are the success criteria well-prioritized and verifiable?: Checked the repository's current test inventory. The `must` criterion saying 'All 5 existing test files pass' is under-scoped because there are more existing relevant tests than the five named in the plan, so it is not a complete yes/no gate for this work.

Are the success criteria well-prioritized and verifiable?: Checked the two user-visible `must` goals for the selection ring and locked-pane chat. They describe the right product outcomes, but they are mostly manual UI behaviors rather than code-review-verifiable gates, so they are not fully testable from the listed automated checks alone.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 1. Weighted score trajectory: 27.5. Plan deltas: n/a. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 0. Open significant flags: 18.

items.criterion: Selection ring (ring-2 ring-sky-400) appears instantly and persistently on selected gallery items — no flicker on hover, no disappearance after click
All 5 existing test files pass without modification to test expectations (unless test was wrong)
AgentChat bubble and panel are visible and functional when editor pane is in locked/pinned state
Multi-drag from gallery to timeline continues working (drag preview, drop handling, track creation)
Edge function changes are backward-compatible — no breaking changes to session schema or tool call format
dragDrop.test.ts (24 tests) all pass after refactoring
No duplicated isRecord/asTrimmedString/asPositiveNumber across server-side edge function files
useExternalDrop.ts onTimelineDrop handler reduced from ~297 lines to a dispatcher under 50 lines
loop.ts runAgentLoop function reduced from 213 lines to under 100 lines of orchestration
command-parser.ts parseCommand uses dispatch map instead of 15 if-branches
AgentChat.tsx component body under ~200 lines after extracting sub-components and hook
No function exceeds 5 levels of nesting after refactoring
useExternalDrop track creation logic appears once (shared helper), not 3 times
AgentChat works correctly in full editor page, unlocked pane, and locked pane (manual smoke test)
Lasso selection, modifier-click toggle, and single-click selection all work in gallery (manual smoke test)

unresolved_flags.concern: agent-chat-rendering: Plan analysis still frames the locked-pane bug around a generic transformed ancestor and does not explicitly account for `pane.transformClass` sliding the whole editor pane off-screen.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `src/features/editor/components/EditorPaneTab.tsx:146-149` applies `pane.transformClass`, and `src/shared/hooks/useSlidingPane.ts:244-259` returns `-translate-y-full`/`translate-y-full` for hidden top/bottom panes.

unresolved_flags.concern: agent-tool-utils: Deduplication scope misses the actual shared helpers in `tools/create-task.ts` and assumes client/server sharing is impossible even though the repo already shares cross-runtime modules.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/create-task.ts:24-28` and `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/generation.ts:22-32` both define `asTrimmedString` and `asPositiveNumber`, while edge files already import from `src/...` in `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/command-parser.ts:1` and `llm/messages.ts:9`.

unresolved_flags.concern: verification: Planned validation omits existing direct tests for the touched frontend files and the final `npx vitest run` command excludes `supabase/functions/**`, leaving the edge refactor largely unverified.
drop-handling: The proposed `createVisualTrackAtTop` extraction does not match all current callers because file drops create both audio and visual tracks depending on file type.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts:214-223` and `:237-246` hardcode `V*` tracks, but `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts:299-309` derives `A*` vs `V*` from `kind`.
